title: Zankoalmighty roar
wikitext:
[]: Zanko...*almighty roar*             Quote Post by Jayfive » Wed Feb 09, 2011 1:11 pm http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sCihud2D9f4&NR=1 There are many live versions on YT. This is a good one :) Apart from anything else the very idea of a western artist performing the theme tune to any film or tv show that didnt pre-exist as a song in its own right (The Who used on CSI for example) is just absurd. But in Japan you sing it BECAUSE it was the theme to a TV show. And a damn fine one too obv.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Wed Feb 09, 2011 2:20 pm But in western society you have the.... FREEBIRD!

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Feb 09, 2011 2:41 pm I'm trying to download this right now in HD... that's a pretty awesome performance, especially with all of those LIGHTS!

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Feb 09, 2011 3:19 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I'm trying to download this right now in HD... that's a pretty awesome performance, especially with all of those LIGHTS!

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Wed Feb 09, 2011 3:54 pm Its a rollocking good tune all the way through but 1:30 - 2:16 = one of the truly great instrumental breaks ever. Im not quite sold on that 2009 version though.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed Feb 09, 2011 6:12 pm I love how into it the crowd was, apparently that’s why so many concert albums from western bands are from Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sat Feb 12, 2011 2:02 pm Holy shit! That's what I'm going for with The Wall vs. Evangelion!

[]:             Quote Post by FES » Sun Feb 13, 2011 2:21 am So is that Japan's version of Don't Stop Believing or what?

[]: Zanko...*almighty roar*             Quote Post by Jayfive » Wed Feb 09, 2011 1:11 pm http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sCihud2D9f4&NR=1 There are many live versions on YT. This is a good one :) Apart from anything else the very idea of a western artist performing the theme tune to any film or tv show that didnt pre-exist as a song in its own right (The Who used on CSI for example) is just absurd. But in Japan you sing it BECAUSE it was the theme to a TV show. And a damn fine one too obv.
